name: RoboSuite
notes: Modular robot simulation framework maintained by Stanford/UT Austin/NVIDIA GEAR. MuJoCo-based. v1.5 (2024) adds humanoid support, custom robot composition, whole-body controllers, Isaac Sim rendering. 9+ standard tasks, 7 robot models, Gymnasium API.
description:
**RoboSuite** is a modular simulation framework for robot learning, originally developed by Stanford Vision and Learning Lab and now maintained by the **ARISE Initiative (Stanford / UT Austin / NVIDIA GEAR)**.

Built on the **MuJoCo** physics engine with standardized benchmark environments.

**v1.5 (Oct 2024) Highlights**:
- Humanoid robot and diverse embodiment support
- Custom robot composition (programmatic assembly of new designs)
- Composite controllers (IK + operational space + joint-space whole-body control)
- NVIDIA Isaac Sim integration for photorealistic rendering

**Core Features**: 9+ standardized manipulation tasks (Lift, Stack, Nut Assembly, Door, etc.), 7 robot arms + 8 gripper models, multi-modal sensors (RGB, depth, proprioception), Gymnasium API compatible. Sister project **robomimic** for human demo collection and imitation learning.

name: Isaac Sim
notes: NVIDIA's high-performance robot simulation toolkit on Omniverse. OpenUSD-based pipelines for parallel scene generation, domain randomization, sensor simulation, and policy training. Core simulation platform for the GR00T model series.
description:
**NVIDIA Isaac Sim** is a high-performance robot simulation toolkit built on the **Omniverse** platform.

Constructs simulation data pipelines based on **OpenUSD**, supporting large-scale parallel scene generation, domain randomization, physics-grade sensor simulation (RGB-D, LiDAR, IMU, etc.), and RL/imitation learning policy training.

Serves as the core simulation data generation and training/validation platform for NVIDIA's **GR00T** series humanoid robot foundation models. Deeply integrated with Isaac Lab (formerly Isaac Gym) for GPU-accelerated massively parallel simulation.

name: Habitat
notes: Meta AI's high-performance embodied navigation & interaction simulation platform. Habitat 3.0 (2023) supports human-robot collaboration, VR human-in-the-loop, social navigation & rearrangement. HM3D, MatterPort3D, Replica datasets. Annual Habitat Challenge.
description:
**Habitat** is a high-performance embodied navigation and interaction simulation platform from **Meta AI** (formerly Facebook AI).

**Habitat 3.0 (2023)** introduces human-robot collaboration tasks, high-fidelity human avatar simulation (articulated skeletons + surface rendering), human-in-the-loop interaction (VR/keyboard-mouse), and two core collaboration tasks: Social Navigation and Social Rearrangement.

**Architecture**: Habitat-Sim (high-performance 3D simulator supporting HM3D, MatterPort3D, Gibson, Replica scene datasets) + Habitat-Lab (modular task definition & training library) + Habitat Challenge (annual CVPR Embodied AI Workshop benchmark). Fetch robot achieves 8,000+ steps/second.

name: iGibson
notes: StanfordVL interactive 3D home simulation environment (Bullet physics). 15 fully interactive home scenes with manipulable objects (doors, drawers, switches). v2.2 (2024) adds Tiago robot, ROS integration. Successor: OmniGibson (NVIDIA Omniverse).
description:
**iGibson (Interactive Gibson)** is an interactive 3D home simulation environment developed by Stanford Vision and Learning Lab.

Based on the **Bullet** physics engine, providing 15 fully interactive home scenes (doors, drawers, switches, furniture, etc.) for robot navigation, manipulation, and interaction tasks.

**v2.2 (2024)** added Tiago robot support, ROS integration, object-centric action primitives, and primitive action interface.

**Successor Project**: **OmniGibson** (built on NVIDIA Omniverse) is the team's current next-generation platform, supporting higher-quality rendering and physics simulation.

name: SAPIEN
institution: 斯坦福大学、上海交通大学
notes: UCSD Hao Su Lab's GPU-parallelized robotics simulation & rendering platform. ManiSkill3 (RSS 2025) achieves 30,000+ FPS, 20+ robot morphologies. SAPIEN 3.0 engine with heterogeneous massively parallel simulation. pip install mani_skill.
description:
**SAPIEN** is an interactive 3D part-level simulation platform developed by UCSD Hao Su Lab, and **ManiSkill** built on top of it is the state-of-the-art GPU-parallelized robotics simulation and benchmark framework.

**ManiSkill3 (RSS 2025) Key Features**:
- GPU-parallel simulation + rendering at **30,000+ FPS** (RGB-D + segmentation)
- 2-3x better GPU memory efficiency than Isaac Lab (128 parallel envs: 4.4GB vs 14.1GB)
- 12 environment categories, **20+ robots** (humanoids, mobile manipulators, quadrupeds, dexterous hands, etc.)
- Built-in ReplicaCAD, AI2THOR scenes, heterogeneous parallel simulation support
- RL (PPO/SAC/TD-MPC2), imitation learning (BC/Diffusion Policy), VLA (Octo/RDT/RT-x) baselines

SAPIEN 3.0 engine provides 2,000+ high-quality 3D models with part-level articulation. Install: pip install --upgrade mani_skill.

name: CoppeliaSim
notes: Cross-platform robot simulation environment (formerly V-REP). V4.10.0 (May 2025), MuJoCo V3.2.6 integration for 2-10x speedup. Multi-engine support (Bullet/ODE/Vortex/Newton). Rich model library & sensor simulation. RLBench backend.
description:
**CoppeliaSim** (formerly V-REP) is a cross-platform general-purpose robot simulation environment developed and maintained by Coppelia Robotics.

**Latest V4.10.0 (May 2025)**, featuring MuJoCo V3.2.6 integration for **2-10x simulation speedup** and new adhesion functionality.

**Core Features**: Multiple physics engine support (Bullet, ODE, Vortex, Newton, MuJoCo), rich robot model library and sensor simulation (vision, depth, force, IMU, etc.), Python/C/C++/MATLAB/ROS multi-language APIs. Serves as the backend simulation platform for **RLBench** and is widely used in robot learning and control research.

name: PyTouch
notes: Meta AI's first ML library for touch processing. PyTorch-based with pre-trained models for touch detection, slip detection, object pose estimation, and contact area estimation. Supports DIGIT, OmniTact, GelSight. pip install pytouch.
description:
**PyTouch** is Meta AI's (Facebook Research) first dedicated machine learning library for tactile sensing.

Built on **PyTorch** with the following pre-trained models:
- Touch/Contact Detection
- Slip Detection
- Object Pose Estimation
- Contact Area Estimation

Supports **DIGIT, OmniTact, GelSight** and other major optical tactile sensors. Companion **TACTO** tactile simulator generates synthetic data for model training.
Install: pip install pytouch.

dataContent.tasks: Tactile perception (contact detection, slip detection, pose estimation)

name: Gibson
notes: StanfordVL realistic 3D scene simulation environment. Indoor scenes from real 3D scans for navigation, perception & interaction. Used with Matterport3D. iGibson is the interactive successor. MIT licensed.
description:
**Gibson Environment (GibsonEnv)** is a realistic 3D scene simulation platform developed by Stanford Vision and Learning Lab.

Reconstructs indoor scenes from real 3D scan data, with RGB, depth, semantic segmentation, and other sensor simulation. Used alongside the **Matterport3D** dataset as a classic simulation environment in embodied navigation.

**Successors**: **iGibson** (adds physical interaction + object manipulation) and **OmniGibson** (next-gen platform built on NVIDIA Omniverse). MIT licensed open source.

name: robomimic
institution: ARISE Initiative (Stanford / UT Austin / NVIDIA GEAR)
notes: Imitation learning data processing framework. Converts raw robot data → standardized HDF5. Dataset conversion, observation extraction, splitting, download management. v0.5.0 (June 2025) adds Diffusion Policy, multi-dataset training, language-conditioned policies. Standard format for BridgeData V2, RoboTurk.
description:
**robomimic** is the premier robot imitation learning data processing and training framework maintained by the ARISE Initiative — one of the most central data tooling pieces in the embodied AI ecosystem.

**Data Processing Pipeline** (raw data → training-ready):
1. Collect human demos (robosuite's collect_human_demonstrations.py)
2. Format conversion (convert_robosuite.py → robomimic-compatible HDF5)
3. Observation extraction (dataset_states_to_obs.py, replay MuJoCo states to generate image observations)
4. Dataset splitting (split_train_val.py, 90-10 split)
5. Training (train.py, supports BC/BC-RNN/BC-Transformer/Diffusion Policy/IQL)

**HDF5 Standard Structure**: demo_N → states/actions/rewards/dones/obs/next_obs, actions normalized to [-1, 1], images as uint8 channel-last (H, W, C).

**v0.5.0 (June 2025)**: Diffusion Policy, multi-dataset training, language-conditioned policies.
v0.4.0 (March 2025): robosuite v1.5 compatibility, datasets migrated to HuggingFace.

Install: pip install robomimic
GitHub: https://github.com/ARISE-Initiative/robomimic

dataContent.scenes: Robot manipulation data from robosuite + custom environments
dataContent.tasks: Format conversion, observation extraction, dataset management, policy training

name: TACTO
notes: Meta AI's open-source optical tactile sensor simulator. Generates synthetic tactile data (RGB+depth, >100FPS) for DIGIT, OmniTact, GelSight. PyBullet-based. Companion simulator to PyTouch. pip install tacto.
description:
**TACTO** is an open-source optical tactile sensor simulator developed by Meta AI (Facebook Research), published in IEEE RA-L 2022.

**Core Capabilities**:
- High-speed rendering: **>100 FPS** high-resolution tactile images (RGB + depth)
- Multi-sensor support: **DIGIT, OmniTact, GelSight** — three major optical tactile sensors
- Physics engine integration: native **PyBullet** support, extensible to other engines
- Modular design: configurable sensor geometry (size, resolution, lighting)

**Relationship with PyTouch**: TACTO generates synthetic tactile training data → PyTouch processes real tactile data. Together they form a complete tactile perception toolchain.

Install: pip install tacto
Community: touch-sensing.org #TACTO channel

dataFormat.schema: sensor config → PyBullet simulation → RGB + depth tactile images

dataContent.scenes: Tactile interaction in PyBullet simulation environments
dataContent.tasks: Synthetic tactile data generation (RGB tactile images + depth maps)
annotations: Auto-generated tactile images and contact information

name: Foxglove / Lichtblick
notes: Robotics data visualization & debugging platform. MCAP/rosbag playback, 3D scenes, time-series plotting. Went closed-source (BUSL-1.1) after Feb 2024. Open-source fork: Lichtblick (BMW, Apache 2.0). MCAP is the default ROS 2 storage format.
description:
**Foxglove** is the leading robotics data visualization and debugging platform, natively supporting **MCAP** format (ROS 2 Iron+ default storage) recording, playback, and analysis.

**Features**: 3D scene visualization, time-series plotting, multi-file merged playback, H.264/H.265 video decoding, Jupyter Notebook integration. Supports ROS 1/2 bag → MCAP conversion.

**License Status**: After February 2024 (v1.87.0), relicensed under **BUSL-1.1 (commercial)**. Free tier provides basic viewing; Pro plans from $20/month.

**Open-Source Successors**:
- **Lichtblick** (maintained by BMW): GitHub 692⭐, Apache 2.0, most active open-source fork. Desktop + browser support, compatible with Foxglove bridge protocol.
  Download: https://github.com/Lichtblick-Suite/lichtblick
- **Trillium** (Robotnik Automation): MPL 2.0, ~40% smaller build, more resource-friendly.
  Download: https://github.com/RobotnikAutomation/trillium

MCAP CLI tools remain Apache 2.0 open source: https://github.com/foxglove/mcap
